audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for temple prediction, Olivet discourse, birth-pains, persecution, abomination/desolation, false messiahs, cosmic signs, Son of Humanity, fig tree, this generation, unknown day/hour, Noah, watchfulness, faithful servant.

verses.restored: Jesus went out from the temple and was going away, and his disciples came to point out the temple buildings to him.
He answered them, Do you not see all these things? Amen, I say to you: not one stone will be left here on another that will not be thrown down.
As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came privately, saying, Tell us, when will these things be, and what is the sign of your arrival/presence and of the completion of the age?
Jesus answered, See that no one leads you astray.
For many will come in my name, saying, I am the Messiah, and they will lead many astray.
You will hear of wars and reports of wars. See that you are not troubled, for it must happen, but the end is not yet.
Nation will rise against nation, and reign against reign, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
All these are the beginning of birth-pains.
Then they will hand you over to pressure and kill you, and you will be hated by all nations because of my name.
Then many will stumble, hand one another over, and hate one another.
Many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
Because lawlessness increases, the love of the many will grow cold.
But the one enduring to the end will be saved.
This good message of the reign will be proclaimed in the whole inhabited world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
Therefore when you see the abomination of desolation spoken of through Daniel the prophet standing in the holy place, let the reader understand,
then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
The one on the roof must not go down to take things from the house,
and the one in the field must not turn back to take the cloak.
Woe to those carrying in the womb and nursing infants in those days.
Pray that your flight may not happen in winter or on a Sabbath.
For then there will be great pressure such as has not happened from the beginning of the world until now, nor ever will happen.
Unless those days were shortened, no flesh would be saved; but because of the chosen, those days will be shortened.
Then if anyone says to you, Look, here is the Messiah, or, Here, do not trust it.
For false messiahs and false prophets will arise and give great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the chosen.
Look, I have told you beforehand.
Therefore if they say to you, Look, he is in the wilderness, do not go out; Look, in the inner rooms, do not trust it.
For just as lightning comes out from east and shines to west, so will be the arrival/presence of the Son of Humanity.
Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures/eagles will be gathered.
Immediately after the pressure of those days, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
Then the sign of the Son of Humanity will appear in heaven, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn; they will see the Son of Humanity coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
He will send his messengers with a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other.
From the fig tree learn the parable: when its branch becomes tender and puts out leaves, you know summer is near.
So also, when you see all these things, know that it is near, at the doors.
Amen, I say to you: this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things happen.
Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
Concerning that day and hour, no one knows, not the messengers of the heavens nor the Son, but the Father alone.
As the days of Noah were, so will be the arrival/presence of the Son of Humanity.
For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark,
and they did not know until the flood came and took all away, so also will be the arrival/presence of the Son of Humanity.
Then two will be in the field; one is taken and one is left.
Two women will be grinding at the mill; one is taken and one is left.
Therefore keep watch, because you do not know on what day your Lord comes.
Know this: if the householder had known in what watch the thief comes, he would have kept watch and not allowed his house to be broken into.
Because of this, you also be ready, for the Son of Humanity comes at an hour you do not think.
Who then is the faithful and wise servant whom the lord appointed over his household to give them food in season?
Blessed/flourishing is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes.
Amen, I say to you: he will appoint him over all his possessions.
But if that harmful servant says in his heart, My lord delays,
and begins to strike his fellow servants and eats and drinks with drunkards,
the lord of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not know,
and will cut him off and place his portion with the hypocrites; there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
